# ParaBank Test Coverage Report

**Ground Truth:** `Parabank.md` (169 test cases across 13 modules)
**Generated Tests:** `1780678546315_Parabank-openai-gpt-5-mini-tests.md` (230 test cases across 13 modules)
**Generated by:** openai/gpt-5-mini | **Report Date:** 2026-05-25

---

## Coverage Definition

> **Missing coverage** = An entire scenario or behaviour that is completely absent from the generated tests but is present in the ground truth.
>
> **Partial negative coverage is acceptable** — if the ground truth tests multiple individual-field empty/invalid cases and the generated tests cover at least one representative field of the same type, that counts as covered. Similarly, if the ground truth tests one dropdown and the generated tests test a different dropdown of the same type, that is still acceptable coverage.
>
> **Extra** = scenarios present in the generated tests but not in the ground truth.

## Module-by-Module Analysis

---

### 1. Login

**Ground Truth:** 12 tests | **Generated:** 12 tests

**Coverage: ✅ Good**

The generated tests cover:
- Valid login with credentials (TC-001) ✅
- Invalid email format (TC-006) ✅
- Empty email field (TC-003) ✅
- Empty password field (TC-004) ✅
- All fields empty (TC-005) ✅
- Password policy violations — min length representative (TC-007) ✅
- Authentication failure / wrong credentials / unregistered email (TC-008) ✅
- Boundary: password exactly at minimum length (TC-009) ✅
- Boundary: password one char below minimum (TC-010) ✅
- Password missing special char (TC-011) ✅
- Whitespace trimming edge (TC-012) ✅

#### ❌ Missing Scenarios

| GT ID | Missing Scenario | Reason |
|-------|-----------------|--------|
| MW-LOGIN-002 | **Valid login with username** (distinct from email login) | The generated tests only cover email login (TC-001). The ground truth explicitly tests that a plain username (non-email) also logs in. The generated tests have no test for this alternate login path. |

> **Note:** Password policy individual violations (uppercase-only, lowercase-only, number-only) are covered by TC-007 as a representative case per the coverage rules — not individually missing.

#### ➕ Extra Scenarios (in Generated, not in GT)

- TC-009: Password exactly at minimum length boundary — more precise boundary than GT
- TC-010: Password one character below minimum — explicit boundary
- TC-012: Email with leading/trailing whitespace trimming — not in GT at all

### 2. Register

**Ground Truth:** 23 tests | **Generated:** 22 tests

**Coverage: ✅ Good**

The generated tests cover all major categories:
- Successful registration (TC-001) ✅
- 9+4 ZIP format (TC-002 ≈ MW-REG-013) ✅
- Phone and SSN auto-formatting (TC-003 ≈ MW-REG-004/005) ✅
- All fields empty (TC-005 ≈ MW-REG-022) ✅
- Representative text field blank — First Name (TC-006 ≈ MW-REG-006) ✅
- State dropdown unselected (TC-007 ≈ MW-REG-010) ✅
- ZIP format violation (TC-008 ≈ MW-REG-012) ✅
- Phone format violation (TC-009 ≈ MW-REG-015) ✅
- SSN format violation (TC-010 ≈ MW-REG-017) ✅
- Username (email) invalid format (TC-011 ≈ MW-REG-018) ✅
- Password < 8 chars (TC-012 ≈ MW-REG-019) ✅
- Confirm password mismatch (TC-013 ≈ MW-REG-020) ✅
- Boundary tests: password exactly 8 chars (TC-014/TC-004), ZIP 5 digits (TC-016), phone 10 digits (TC-018), SSN 9 digits (TC-020) ✅

> Individual field empty cases (Last Name, Street Address, City, Phone, SSN, Confirm Password) from GT are covered by the representative field approach (TC-006 covers "blank text field" class). Per coverage rules, this is acceptable.

#### ❌ Missing Scenarios

None — all scenario types covered.

#### ➕ Extra Scenarios (in Generated, not in GT)

- TC-021: Browser Back after successful registration — form shows blank (not in GT)

### 5. Transfer Funds

**Coverage: ⚠️ Partial**

Well-covered areas:

- Insufficient funds (TC-009 ≈ MW-TF-008) ✅
- External account mismatch (TC-011 ≈ MW-TF-010) ✅
- No source selected (TC-006 ≈ MW-TF-011) ✅
- No destination selected (TC-010 ≈ MW-TF-012) ✅

- Minimum transfer $0.01 (TC-015 ≈ MW-TF-014) ✅

#### ❌ Missing Scenarios

| GT ID | Missing Scenario | Reason |
|-------|-----------------|--------|
| MW-TF-009 | **Same source and destination account** for internal transfer | The generated tests cover "no destination selected" (TC-010) and "mismatched external accounts" (TC-011), but never test the case where a user selects the same account for both source and destination in an internal transfer. This is a distinct business rule. |

#### ➕ Extra Scenarios (in Generated, not in GT)

- TC-004: Transfer type (radio) left blank
- TC-007: Non-numeric transfer amount
- TC-012: External account number fields left blank (conditional required)
- TC-016: Zero amount specifically for external transfer
- TC-018: External confirmation differs by one character (boundary)

### 6. Payments

**Ground Truth:** 15 tests | **Generated:** 14 tests

**Coverage: ⚠️ Partial**

Well-covered areas:
- Successful payment (TC-001 ≈ MW-BP-001) ✅
- Account numbers mismatch (TC-004 ≈ MW-BP-012) ✅
- Insufficient funds (TC-005 ≈ MW-BP-014) ✅
- No source account (TC-006 ≈ MW-BP-015) ✅
- Non-numeric amount (TC-003 ≈ implicit amount validation) ✅
- Balance updated after payment (TC-001 expected result ≈ MW-BP-003) ✅

#### ❌ Missing Scenarios

| GT ID | Missing Scenario | Reason |
|-------|-----------------|--------|
| MW-BP-002 | **Quick select / pre-fill payee** from preset payees (Electric Company, Gas Utility, Internet Provider) | The generated tests have no test for the quick-select payee feature where selecting a pre-set payee auto-fills all payee fields. This entire feature scenario is absent. |
| MW-BP-004 through MW-BP-011 (as a class) | **Individual empty-field validation for payee form fields** | The generated tests (TC-003–TC-006) only test amount non-numeric, account mismatch, insufficient funds, and no source. There is no test covering empty Payee Name, Street Address, City, State, ZIP, or Phone fields individually or as a group. The ground truth has 8 separate empty-field tests; the generated tests have zero empty-field coverage for the payee details section. |

> Per coverage rules, one representative empty-field test would suffice — but the generated tests have **none** for the payee fields block.

#### ➕ Extra Scenarios (in Generated, not in GT)

- TC-002: Alternate source account payment
- TC-007/TC-008: Boundary: amount = exact balance / one unit above balance
- TC-009/TC-010: Account number exact match / one-char diff boundary
- TC-011: Whitespace in confirm account number
- TC-012: Excessive decimal precision on payment amount
- TC-013: Rapid re-submission / duplicate prevention via back button

**Ground Truth:** 18 tests | **Generated:** 32 tests

**Coverage: ✅ Good**

- Below minimum for all three types (TC-015/016/017/024/026/028 ≈ MW-RL-005/007/009) ✅
- Above maximum for all types (TC-015/016/017/030 ≈ MW-RL-006/008/010) ✅
- Down payment ≥ loan amount (TC-018 ≈ MW-RL-011) ✅
- Insufficient collateral < 20% (TC-020/032 ≈ MW-RL-012) ✅
- Down payment < 10% (TC-019 ≈ MW-RL-013) ✅
- No loan type selected (TC-011 ≈ MW-RL-014) ✅
- No collateral account (TC-013 ≈ MW-RL-015) ✅
- Exact minimum boundaries (TC-023/025/027/031 ≈ MW-RL-016/017/018) ✅

#### ❌ Missing Scenarios

None — all scenario types covered. Generated tests are significantly more comprehensive.

#### ➕ Extra Scenarios (in Generated, not in GT)

- TC-004–TC-010: Denial scenarios with specific denial reasons surfaced
- TC-014/TC-021/TC-022: All fields empty, non-positive loan amount, non-numeric amount
- TC-025/TC-026: Auto loan exact minimum / one-below-minimum boundary
- TC-027/TC-028: Home loan exact minimum / one-below boundary
- TC-029/TC-030: Home loan exact maximum / one-above-maximum boundary

### 8. Update Contact Info

**Ground Truth:** 18 tests | **Generated:** 12 tests

**Coverage: ⚠️ Partial**

Well-covered areas:
- Pre-populated form (TC-001 pre-condition ≈ MW-UCI-001) ✅
- Successful full update (TC-001 ≈ MW-UCI-002) ✅
- Update phone only (TC-002 ≈ MW-UCI-009) ✅
- Required field blank — representative First Name (TC-004 ≈ MW-UCI-010) ✅
- All fields empty (TC-005 ≈ MW-UCI-022-equivalent) ✅
- Invalid ZIP format (TC-006 ≈ MW-UCI-017) ✅
- Invalid phone format (TC-007 ≈ MW-UCI-018) ✅

#### ❌ Missing Scenarios

| GT ID | Missing Scenario | Reason |
|-------|-----------------|--------|
| MW-UCI-003 through MW-UCI-009 | **Individual field update tests** (First Name, Last Name, Address, City, State, ZIP, Phone each updated in isolation) | The generated tests cover full-form update (TC-001) and phone-only update (TC-002), but do not test updating each field independently. The ground truth has 7 individual field update tests. While this might seem granular, the ground truth treats each field update as a separate functional scenario — the generated tests collapse all of these into one test, missing the validation that individual field saves work correctly in isolation for fields like First Name, Last Name, City, State. |

> **Note:** This is flagged because the ground truth's intent is clearly to test each editable field can be saved independently. The generated tests only cover full-form and phone. Missing: First Name update, Last Name update, Address update, City update, State update, ZIP update as individual save operations.

#### ➕ Extra Scenarios (in Generated, not in GT)

- TC-008: Invalid format in Last Name field
- TC-003: Submit without any changes (refresh/no-op update)
- TC-009: Very long First Name input (200+ chars)
- TC-010: Last Name with special characters / emoji
- TC-011: Leading/trailing whitespace in Street Address
- TC-012: Rapid re-submission via browser back

### 9. Manage Cards

**Ground Truth:** 10 tests | **Generated:** 18 tests

**Coverage: ⚠️ Partial**

Well-covered areas:
- Request Debit card (TC-001 ≈ MW-MC-001 — Card Type is a dropdown so debit/credit type both covered) ✅
- Update spending limit (TC-002 ≈ MW-MC-005) ✅
- Add travel notice / travel dates (TC-003 ≈ MW-MC-006) ✅
- Freeze card (TC-002 status → Frozen ≈ MW-MC-007) ✅
- Incomplete address (TC-005 ≈ MW-MC-003) ✅
- Invalid spending limit (TC-008 ≈ MW-MC-009) ✅
- Invalid date range (TC-009 ≈ MW-MC-010) ✅

#### ❌ Missing Scenarios

| GT ID | Missing Scenario | Reason |
|-------|-----------------|--------|
| MW-MC-004 | **No account selected** for card request | The generated tests cover incomplete address (TC-005) and account not in good standing (TC-006), but do not test leaving the "Account to Link" dropdown completely unselected without any other error. TC-006 is about bad standing, which is distinct from the field being empty/unselected. |
| MW-MC-008 | **Unfreeze card** (Frozen → Active transition) | The generated tests cover freezing a card (TC-002, status → Frozen) and block disallowed transitions (TC-010). However, there is no explicit test for unfreezing — i.e., selecting a currently Frozen card and changing status back to Active. This is a distinct positive workflow. |

#### ➕ Extra Scenarios (in Generated, not in GT)

- TC-004: Multiple travel destinations
- TC-006: Account not in good standing blocks card request
- TC-007: Non-numeric spending limit
- TC-010: Disallowed card status transition
- TC-011/TC-012: Spending limit at/above policy limit boundary
- TC-013/TC-014: Travel notice same-day / one-day-after boundary
- TC-015/TC-016: Very long / whitespace-padded shipping address
- TC-017: Remove all destinations from travel notice, then update
- TC-018: Rapid re-submission after card request

### 13. Support Center

**Ground Truth:** 11 tests | **Generated:** 19 tests

**Coverage: ⚠️ Partial**

Well-covered areas:
- Send message successfully (TC-001 ≈ MW-SC-001) ✅
- Send with attachment (TC-002 ≈ MW-SC-002) ✅
- Empty message body (TC-004 ≈ MW-SC-004) ✅
- Invalid attachment type (TC-010 ≈ MW-SC-005) ✅
- Schedule callback (TC-003 ≈ MW-SC-007) ✅
- Phone pre-filled (implicit in TC-003 precondition ≈ MW-SC-008) ✅
- Date too soon / not next business day (TC-011/014 ≈ MW-SC-009) ✅
- Invalid phone format (TC-012 ≈ MW-SC-010) ✅

#### ❌ Missing Scenarios

| GT ID | Missing Scenario | Reason |
|-------|-----------------|--------|
| MW-SC-003 | **Empty Subject field** validation | The generated tests test empty Message Body (TC-004) and a very long Subject (TC-015), but do not test submitting with Subject left empty. The ground truth explicitly tests Subject as a required field. The generated TC-015 tests max length, not whether the field is required when left blank. |
| MW-SC-006 | **Category dropdown options** (Account, Technical, Security, Other) | The generated tests select a category as part of positive flows but never explicitly verify the contents/options of the Category dropdown (GT: MW-SC-006 checks that exactly these 4 options appear). No generated test validates the dropdown option set. |

#### ➕ Extra Scenarios (in Generated, not in GT)

- TC-005/TC-006/TC-007: Leave Reason for Call / Preferred Date / Preferred Time Window blank (individual)
- TC-008: Clear pre-filled phone number (blank)
- TC-009: All callback fields empty simultaneously
- TC-013/TC-014: Preferred Date exactly next business day / one day before (boundary)
- TC-016: Message body contains only whitespace
- TC-017: Phone number whitespace trimming
- TC-018/TC-019: Rapid re-submission duplicate prevention (message and callback)

---

## Overall Findings

### Missing Coverage — Consolidated List

| # | Module | Missing Scenario | Impact |
|---|--------|-----------------|--------|
| 1 | Login | Valid login with username (non-email) | Medium — alternate login path untested |
| 2 | Accounts Overview | Welcome/greeting message displayed | Low — cosmetic but in GT |
| 3 | Transfer Funds | Same source and destination account | High — distinct business rule |
| 4 | Payments | Quick select / pre-fill payee | Medium — full feature absent |
| 5 | Payments | Empty-field validation for payee form fields | High — zero coverage for payee detail validation |
| 6 | Update Contact Info | Individual field update tests (7 fields) | Medium — only full-form and phone-only covered |
| 7 | Manage Cards | No account selected for card request | Medium — distinct from account-in-bad-standing |
| 8 | Manage Cards | Unfreeze card (Frozen → Active) | Medium — positive unfreeze path absent |
| 9 | Support Center | Empty Subject validation | Medium — required field not tested empty |
| 10 | Support Center | Category dropdown options verification | Low — dropdown content not validated |

### Generated Tests Strengths

The generated tests significantly exceed the ground truth in these areas:
- **Boundary testing** — Every module has explicit ±1 unit boundary tests that the GT largely omits
- **Edge cases** — Whitespace trimming, excessive decimal precision, emoji/unicode inputs
- **Interaction edges** — Browser back + resubmit / duplicate prevention (not tested at all in GT)
- **Empty-form catch-all** — "Submit with ALL fields empty" test in every module
- **Request Loan** — 32 tests vs 18 in GT, covering all six loan boundary points (min/max per type)
- **Account Statements** — Thorough conditional field tests for the period-type toggle
- **Investments** — Start-date-today boundary, far-future date, exact-share-balance sell

---

| Login | 92% | Username login path missing |
| Register | 100% | All scenarios covered |

| Request Loan | 100% | All scenarios covered, heavily extended |
| Update Contact Info | 85% | Individual field update tests missing |
| Manage Cards | 80% | No-account-selected + unfreeze missing |
| Investments | 100% | All scenarios covered |
| Account Statements | 100% | All scenarios covered |
| Security Settings | 100% | All scenarios covered |
| Support Center | 82% | Empty subject + category dropdown options missing |
| **Overall** | **~91%** | 9 missing scenario types across 13 modules |
